trivia_bullets.text: Energy counters originated in Kaladesh (2016). MH3 recontextualized energy as 'the power of the Roil' on Zendikar, decoupling the resource from its Kaladesh-technology flavor and applying it across multiple planes and creature types. [magic.wizards.com: 'Modern Horizons 3 Mechanics']

Cyclops Superconductor packs three distinct mechanical roles onto a single common: Prowess, an enter-the-battlefield energy bank (three counters), and a conditional death-trigger damage spell. This design density — triple-function on a common 2/2 for {1}{U}{R} — is notable for the rarity. [Scryfall; MTG Arena Zone]

EDHREC tracks approximately 2,990 Commander decks including Cyclops Superconductor. It occupies a modest niche in energy-based Commander builds, where sacrifice enablers can bank the entry energy and weaponize the death-trigger. [EDHREC: cyclops-superconductor via WebSearch]
sources: EDHREC: cyclops-superconductor card page (via WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Cyclops Wizard is a rare type combination. MTG cyclops have historically been red-green aggressive creatures; the Wizard subtype here signals cross-archetype synergies (Wizard payoffs, energy synergies, prowess triggers) consistent with MH3's layered creature-type design philosophy. [Scryfall type line via WebSearch]

related_cards.note: EDHREC frequent co-inclusion in Cyclops Superconductor energy builds
